rulings.Calendar Matter: Insurance Commissioners Amended Notice Of Application And Application For Order Approving Financial Report And Expenses Of Administration; Financial Report On Status Of Castlepoint National Insurance Company In Liquidation; Memo Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 13, 2020, Line 1, PETITIONER DAVE JONES Insurance Commissioners Amended Notice Of Application And Application For Order Approving Financial Report And Expenses Of Administration; Financial Report On Status Of Castlepoint National Insurance Company In Liquidation: The Insurance Commissioner's application for an order approving financial report and expenses of administration, as supplemented on July 13, 2020, is granted. No opposition filed, and good cause shown. Non-party Christopher L. Dion's "limited objection" to the application is not properly before the Court. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the Commissioner is required to prepare a proposed order and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN HUGHES VS. GOLDEN GATE SCENIC STEAMSHIP CORP., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Lien
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 13, 2020, Line 3, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE SCENIC STEAMSHIP CORP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Lien: Defendant Golden Gate Scenic Steamship Corporation's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication is denied. First, the motion is procedurally improper under Code of Civil Procedure § 437c(f)(1). Defendant's notice of motion does not seek to adjudicate or completely dispose of any "cause of action," "affirmative defense," "claim for damages," or "issue of duty." Second, and more importantly, defendant's argument fails as a matter of law. If an insurer makes voluntary LHWCA payments to an injured worker on behalf of the employer, the insurer has a subrogation lien on the worker's Jones Act recovery for the amount of LHWCA benefits paid and has a right to intervene in the worker's Jones Act lawsuit. (Chenevert v. Travelers Indemnity Co., 746 F.3d 581, 588 (5th Cir. 2014); see also Kahue v. Pacific Environmental Corp. (D.Haw. 2011) 834 F.Supp.2d 1039, 1060-1061.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the lien claimant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2020, Line 1, Defendant Intero Real Estate Services, Inc, Wachiraporn "Jane" Wachiradejkul, Douglas Tobin's Motion For Evidentiary Sanctions Against Eagle Defendants For Spoliation Of Evidence. (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem.(end of 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HILARY WARE ET AL VS. EAGLE PROPERTY SOLUTIONS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Intero Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidentiary Sanctions Against Eagle Defendants For Spoliation Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2020, Line 1, DEFENDANT INTERO REAL ESTATE SERVICES, INC, WACHIRAPORN "JANE" WACHIRADEJKUL, DOUGLAS TOBIN's Motion For Evidentiary Sanctions Against Eagle Defendants For Spoliation Of Evidence. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties are reminded that courtesy copies of papers filed on discovery motions must be emailed to the discovery department. Intero Real Estate Services et al. (Intero) requested that Eagle Property Solutions et al. (Eagle) produce an email dated November 15, 2016 in native format with all metadata intact. Eagle responded that it was unable to do so because it was "no longer able to access the email account marion@eaglepsllc.com  used to send [the email] in 2016," and "[a]s a result  was unable to provide [the email] in the format requested." Intero asserts that Eagle has spoliated evidence and seeks evidentiary sanctions in the form of an order establishing that Eagle "never sent the email" and that the November 15, 2016 timestamp on the email "is a forgery." The email in question purports to have been sent on November 15, 2016 (seven months before the action was filed) from "marion@eaglepsllc.com" to "janewsfo@gmail.com," "mmatundan@icloud.com" and "Jenae.lozado@yahoo.com." It appears to have been subsequently forwarded on July 2, 2019 (more than two years after the action was filed) from "marion@eaglepsllc.com" to "marionmatundan@outlook.com." Eagle asserts that eleven months later, in June 2020, it no longer had access to the email account "marion@eaglepsllc.com," but offers no explanation how or why access to the account was "lost" notwithstanding Eagle's obligation to preserve evidence relevant to the pending litigation. Neither party discusses whether the original email is available in native format at janewsfo@gmail.com, mmatundan@icloud.com, Jenae.lozado@yahoo.com or marionmatundan@outlook.com. The record on this motion is insufficient to establish spoliation or to determine what remedy, if any, is appropriate. "[I]n most cases of purported spoliation the facts should be decided and any appropriate inference should be made by the trier of fact after a full hearing at trial." New Albertsons, Inc. v. Superior Court, 168 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1431 (2008). The motion is denied, without prejudice to a motion in limine to the trial court, which will be able to evaluate the issues presented on a fuller record and fashion a remedy, if warranted.(end of 1 of 2)=(302/JPT)
